summary: No-ship: setup can clone/sync the license-isolated repo inside the Garrison tree before aborting, and start can leave the child server orphaned on startup/error paths.

findings.title: License isolation guard runs after cloning and syncing external code
findings.recommendation: Move the license-isolation check before `mkdir -p`, `git clone`, `git checkout`, and `uv sync`; preferably resolve the repo root dynamically instead of relying only on path globs, then abort before any external bytes are written under the MIT tree.

findings.title: Supervisor exits without reaping or escalating child shutdown on startup/error failures
findings.recommendation: Make cleanup wait for the child `exit` event before parent exit, with a timeout and SIGKILL escalation. Also wrap `main()` in a catch/finally cleanup path so mkdir/write/register failures cannot terminate the parent while leaving the spawned server running.
